comment_content: The usual sneering, stereotypical attitude towards Britain and the British, with disparaging talk of 'Jolly old England', bad food, not hugging because of repressed feelings', upper-class buffoons living in country estates, etc,  you get the picture - which is all utter crap by the way.  Oh, and apparently the Thames Barrier stops the Atlantic Ocean from flooding London, according to this nonsense  Really? Well, perhaps they'd like to explain that impossibility, given that the Thames flows into the North Sea, and is nowhere near the Atlantic. If you're American, I'll try to explain the childishness of such stereotypical views that Hollyweird puts forward regarding my country. They are akin to me turning up in the U.S. and expecting everyone to be wearing a 10-gallon hat, wearing a couple of six-shooters, shouting 'yee aww, howdy pardner!' Now wouldn't you find that somewhat pathetic?  It's mildly entertaining - emphasis on mildly - but it gets tiresome after a while, with Foxx and Diaz beating up hoards of baddies with ease, time after time.  If there's a follow-up, I won't be watching it. 🥱
reply_content:
Geeeeez - I read it as being deliberately over the top kind of sending itself up and not taking itself too seriously.  But I’m originally from Australia, lol, where you get the best (and worst lol) of both worlds (US and UK). Honestly I don’t think it was trying to be a bond movie or A FIsh Called Wanda.  It was more understated in the humor (humour 😅 - see what living in the states does to spelling) department, so it was meant to be a lighthearted feel good movie with a bit of “whiz bang”, and some silliness.  I mean come on, Jamie Demetriou was supposed to be awkward lovable and bumbling. 

It wasn’t trying to be a blockbuster or in depth, and it clearly borrowed bits from a variety of well known films. Honestly sometimes there are times for an action lighthearted romp with a bit of silliness.  One person’s meat …. But each to their own. 

My wife and I definitely laughed and enjoyed it in that regard. Sorry you felt such angst about the deliberate silly stereotypes. Maybe they were taking themselves less seriously than others lol. 😅
